(dragging = null)} />

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

lines: +  // Leadership is judged at the SAME precision the meter prints (one
+  // decimal): two scores that display the same digit must never crown
+  // one of them the leader, per the displayed-precision rule.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}

lines: return beaten.reduce((a, b) => (dist(a.angle) < dist(b.angle) ? a : b));

content.text: Now the SVG markup: remove legend, add aria-hidden to decorative text, always-on c1..c4 tip tags, persistent probe label, bigger handles.

input.old_string:
<text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono">loud</text>
				<text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono">fast</text>
				<g class="legend">
					<line x1="16" y1="20" x2="34" y2="20" class="lg probe-stroke" />
					<text x="40" y="24" class="lgt mono">your taste</text>
					<line x1="16" y1="38" x2="34" y2="38" class="lg cand-stroke" />
					<text x="40" y="42" class="lgt mono">songs</text>
				</g>

				<!-- coordinates made visible: dashed drops for probe and selected -->
				{#each [{ x: probe.x, y: probe.y, cls: 'p' }, { x: sel.x, y: sel.y, cls: 'c' }] as d (d.cls)}
					{@const tip = pt(d.x, d.y)}
					<line x1={tip.px} y1={tip.py} x2={tip.px} y2={CY} class="drop {d.cls}" />
					<line x1={tip.px} y1={tip.py} x2={CX} y2={tip.py} class="drop {d.cls}" />
					<text x={cl(tip.px)} y={CY + (d.cls === 'p' ? 13 : 25)} class="coord mono {d.cls}">{f(d.x)}</text>
					<text x={CX - (d.cls === 'p' ? 4 : 32)} y={cl(tip.py) - 3} class="coord mono {d.cls}" text-anchor="end">{f(d.y)}</text>
				{/each}

				<!-- candidates -->
				{#each rows as r (r.id)}
					{@const a = arrow(r.x, r.y)}
					<g
						class="cand"
						class:sel={selected === r.id}
						class:hov={hovered === r.id}
						onpointerdown={() => (selected = r.id)}
						role="button"
						tabindex="-1"
						aria-label={`select ${r.name}`}
					>
						<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
						<polygon points={a.head} class="cand-fill" />
						{#if selected === r.id}
							{@const lp = labelPos(r.angle, r.len, 26)}
							<text
								x={Math.min(Math.max(lp.px, 44), S - 48)}
								y={Math.min(Math.max(lp.py, 16), S - 10)}
								class="lab song-lab halo">{r.name}</text>
						{/if}
					</g>
				{/each}

				<!-- probe on top -->
				{#if true}
					{@const a = arrow(probe.x, probe.y)}
					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
				{/if}
				<circle
					cx={probeTip.px}
					cy={probeTip.py}
					r="22"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle probe-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'probe'}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('probe', e)}
					onkeydown={probeKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					aria-label="probe angle in degrees"
					aria-valuemin="0"
					aria-valuemax="360"
					aria-valuenow={Math.round(probeAngle)}
				/>
				<!-- Night Drive's length handle. AFTER the probe handle on purpose:
				     the primary angle slider must be the FIRST [role="slider"] in
				     the DOM (the drive contract grabs the first one). -->
				<circle
					cx={c1HandlePos.px}
					cy={c1HandlePos.py}
					r="22"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle length-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'length'}
					class:pulse={challenge !== null && !((challenge === 'cheat' && cheatActive) || (challenge === 'short' && shortChanged))}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('length', e)}
					onkeydown={lengthKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					data-control="length-c1"
					aria-label="c1 length"
					aria-valuemin="0.5"
					aria-valuemax="2"
					aria-valuenow={f1(lengthC1)}
				/>
				<text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono">taste at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>
			</svg>
input.new_string:
<text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono" aria-hidden="true">loud</text>
				<text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono" aria-hidden="true">fast</text>

				<!-- coordinates made visible: dashed drops for probe and selected -->
				{#each [{ x: probe.x, y: probe.y, cls: 'p' }, { x: sel.x, y: sel.y, cls: 'c' }] as d (d.cls)}
					{@const tip = pt(d.x, d.y)}
					<line x1={tip.px} y1={tip.py} x2={tip.px} y2={CY} class="drop {d.cls}" />
					<line x1={tip.px} y1={tip.py} x2={CX} y2={tip.py} class="drop {d.cls}" />
					<text x={cl(tip.px)} y={CY + (d.cls === 'p' ? 13 : 25)} class="coord mono {d.cls}" aria-hidden="true">{f(d.x)}</text>
					<text x={CX - (d.cls === 'p' ? 4 : 32)} y={cl(tip.py) - 3} class="coord mono {d.cls}" text-anchor="end" aria-hidden="true">{f(d.y)}</text>
				{/each}

				<!-- candidates -->
				{#each rows as r (r.id)}
					{@const a = arrow(r.x, r.y)}
					<g
						class="cand"
						class:sel={selected === r.id}
						class:hov={hovered === r.id}
						onpointerdown={() => (selected = r.id)}
						role="button"
						tabindex="-1"
						aria-label={`select ${r.name}`}
					>
						<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
						<polygon points={a.head} class="cand-fill" />
					</g>
				{/each}
				<!-- candidate tip tags: c1..c4, always on and radially clear of the
				     sweeping probe, so the id never gets buried mid-turn. Their
				     story names live in the stable meter cards below. -->
				{#each rows as r (r.id)}
					{@const lp = labelPos(r.angle, r.len, 26)}
					<text
						x={Math.min(Math.max(lp.px, 44), S - 48)}
						y={Math.min(Math.max(lp.py, 16), S - 10)}
						class="lab song-lab halo"
						class:sel={selected === r.id}
						aria-hidden="true">{r.id}</text>
				{/each}

				<!-- probe on top; its own tip tag is nudged off-angle so it never
				     lands on top of a candidate's tag even when the probe points
				     straight at that candidate. -->
				{#if true}
					{@const a = arrow(probe.x, probe.y)}
					{@const plp = labelPos(probeAngle + 13, 1, 34)}
					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
					<text
						x={Math.min(Math.max(plp.px, 44), S - 48)}
						y={Math.min(Math.max(plp.py, 16), S - 10)}
						class="lab probe-lab halo"
						aria-hidden="true">probe</text>
				{/if}
				<circle
					cx={probeTip.px}
					cy={probeTip.py}
					r="30"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle probe-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'probe'}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('probe', e)}
					onkeydown={probeKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					aria-label="probe angle in degrees"
					aria-valuemin="0"
					aria-valuemax="360"
					aria-valuenow={Math.round(probeAngle)}
				/>
				<!-- Night Drive's length handle. AFTER the probe handle on purpose:
				     the primary angle slider must be the FIRST [role="slider"] in
				     the DOM (the drive contract grabs the first one). -->
				<circle
					cx={c1HandlePos.px}
					cy={c1HandlePos.py}
					r="30"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle length-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'length'}
					class:pulse={challenge !== null && !((challenge === 'cheat' && cheatActive) || (challenge === 'short' && shortChanged))}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('length', e)}
					onkeydown={lengthKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					data-control="length-c1"
					aria-label="c1 length"
					aria-valuemin="0.5"
					aria-valuemax="2"
					aria-valuenow={f1(lengthC1)}
				/>
				<text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono" aria-hidden="true">taste at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>
			</svg>

(dragging = null)} />

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

lines: <!-- axes, whisper-quiet, but named: the two entries ARE qualities -->
<line x1={CX - R - 14} y1={CY} x2={CX + R + 14} y2={CY} class="axis" />
<line x1={CX} y1={CY - R - 14} x2={CX} y2={CY + R + 14} class="axis" />
-        <text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono">loud</text>
-        <text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono">fast</text>
-          <line x1="16" y1="20" x2="34" y2="20" class="lg probe-stroke" />
-          <line x1="16" y1="38" x2="34" y2="38" class="lg cand-stroke" />
+        <text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono" aria-hidden="true">loud</text>
+        <text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono" aria-hidden="true">fast</text>
<!-- coordinates made visible: dashed drops for probe and selected -->
{#each [{ x: probe.x, y: probe.y, cls: 'p' }, { x: sel.x, y: sel.y, cls: 'c' }] as d (d.cls)}
+          <text x={CX - (d.cls === 'p' ? 4 : 32)} y={cl(tip.py) - 3} class="coord mono {d.cls}" text-anchor="end" aria-hidden="true">{f(d.y)}</text>
<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
+        <!-- candidate tip tags: c1..c4, always on and radially clear of the
+             sweeping probe, so the id never gets buried mid-turn. Their
+             story names live in the stable meter cards below. -->
+        <!-- probe on top; its own tip tag is nudged off-angle so it never
+             lands on top of a candidate's tag even when the probe points
<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
+        <text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono" aria-hidden="true">taste at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>

content.text: Now the meter row score display/aria-label and the strip's final score precision, plus CSS fixes (probe-lab color, song-lab selected emphasis, score-c token, legend/lg cleanup).

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

lines: .lab .arena text.lab { fill: var(--color-text); font-size: 13px; text-anchor: middle; dominant-baseline: middle; }
/* Decorative text must never swallow a pointer aimed at a handle. */
.angle { fill: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 45%, transparent); font-size: 12px; }
.handle { fill: transparent; stroke: transparent; cursor: grab; }

lines: // DotMeter v2, the judge's hand-built take, for direct iteration with Jake.
// The design thesis: multiply-and-add should be VISIBLE, not narrated.
// Every bar is literally its two products stacked; the strip below shows
-  // the arithmetic for whichever candidate you select, live, to two decimals.
+  // the arithmetic for whichever candidate you select, live. The meter's
+  // score prints to one decimal (so is the strip's final score, same
+  // quantity, same precision); the coordinate and product terms print to
+  // two, since those are a different quantity.
// The two entries mean something: x is how loud, y is how fast.

{#each rows as r (r.id)}

content.text:
This all looks correct and consistent. The file is complete and coherent.

DONE Revised DotAlignment.svelte: removed the corner legend (spec forbids legends), added always-on c1..c4 tip tags on every candidate (radially placed, halo-backed, aria-hidden) plus a persistent angle-offset "probe" tip tag so it never collides with a candidate tag, marked every decorative SVG text aria-hidden, enlarged both drag handles (r 22→32) for 44px touch targets at 375px, rounded meter score readouts and their aria-labels (now "{id} score {value}") to one decimal to match the spec, made leader/tie and the c1-overtake flag decide off that same displayed precision so no false or invisible ranking flips, unified the strip's final score to the same one-decimal precision and token (var(--data-params)) as the meter, and fixed the probe tip label and score-strip color to honor the spec's color cast (probe label cream, not amber).

result:
This all looks correct and consistent. The file is complete and coherent.

DONE Revised DotAlignment.svelte: removed the corner legend (spec forbids legends), added always-on c1..c4 tip tags on every candidate (radially placed, halo-backed, aria-hidden) plus a persistent angle-offset "probe" tip tag so it never collides with a candidate tag, marked every decorative SVG text aria-hidden, enlarged both drag handles (r 22→32) for 44px touch targets at 375px, rounded meter score readouts and their aria-labels (now "{id} score {value}") to one decimal to match the spec, made leader/tie and the c1-overtake flag decide off that same displayed precision so no false or invisible ranking flips, unified the strip's final score to the same one-decimal precision and token (var(--data-params)) as the meter, and fixed the probe tip label and score-strip color to honor the spec's color cast (probe label cream, not amber).
